title: Knee Pain Relief: Simple Ways to Feel Better
summary: A big study found that simple things like knee braces, water therapy, and exercise are the best ways to help with knee arthritis pain without using medicine. Knee arthritis makes knees hurt and stiff, and it affects many older people. Medicines can have bad side effects, so doctors looked at other ways. They studied 139 trials with almost 10,000 people and compared 12 treatments. Knee braces were the best at reducing pain and helping knees move better. Water therapy, which is doing exercises in warm water, was great for pain relief. Exercise also helped a lot. Some fancy treatments like laser therapy worked a little, but ultrasound didn't help much. The study says these simple options are safer than drugs and should be tried first.

physical activity to make the body stronger and reduce pain

bad things that can happen when taking medicine

background_read: Knee arthritis is a common problem where the knee joint becomes painful and stiff, often in older adults. It can make walking and other activities hard. Many people take medicines to feel better, but these can sometimes cause stomach or heart problems. Scientists did a big study to find safer ways to help, like using knee braces or doing exercises in water. This study looked at lots of other research to see what works best without drugs.
Article_Structure: WHO: Researchers who studied knee arthritis treatments
WHAT: Found that knee braces, water therapy, and exercise are the best non-drug options for pain relief
WHERE: In studies involving people from many places
WHY: To help patients avoid the risks of medicines and feel better safely

perspectives.description: Patients might feel hopeful because simple, safe options can reduce pain without medicine side effects.
